target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes the Agent Tools / `tools.effective` gap where configured bundled MCP tools could be available at runtime but missing from the session-effective inventory.
- Keeps normal `tools.effective` reads side-effect-light: cold reads do not start/list MCP transports and instead return an explanatory MCP notice.
- Adds explicit `tools.effective.refresh` as an `operator.write` path for live MCP discovery/materialization, then lets warm reads project the cached MCP catalog.
- Updates SDK/protocol/UI/tests so the Control UI shows **Refresh Available Tools** and displays MCP tools after explicit refresh.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #: N/A --- intentionally not closing #76724 because that report also includes reporter-specific SSE/runtime-provider-body details outside this PR's scope.
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation
#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list MCP tools. Runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but the dashboard inventory used a separate static inventory path. This PR keeps the read path safe and explicit: `tools.effective` peeks existing MCP catalog state and returns notices, while `tools.effective.refresh` performs live discovery on user action.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` should not cold-start/list MCP transports from a read-scoped dashboard load, while the dashboard must still have an explicit path to discover configured MCP tools and then show the warm catalog.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from current PR head `f5cfb1a499a30b5d2e091ea47f368d478192fb66`, QA mock provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled. Current base checked locally: `1bc32e53ab1d`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr84096-conflict-20260528-131511 \
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAD=f5cfb1a499a3 \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=1bc32e53ab1d \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-summary-current-f5cfb1a499a3.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-current.ts

node --import tsx /tmp/capture-openclaw-pr84096-ui-proof-current-f5cfb1a499a3.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from the current-head Gateway RPC harness:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "localHead": "f5cfb1a499a3",
  "upstreamCommit": "1bc32e53ab1d",
  "cold": {
    "noticeIds": ["mcp-not-yet-connected"],
    "containsMcpTarget": false,
    "runtimeCreated": false,
    "groups": ["core:19", "plugin:2"]
  },
  "refreshed": {
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "runtimeCreated": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "catalogToolNames": ["probe_tool"],
    "groups": ["core:19", "plugin:2", "mcp:1"]
  },
  "warm": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groups": ["core:19", "plugin:2", "mcp:1"]
  }
}
```

Current-head Control UI browser proof:

```text
Cold Agent Tools panel:
- shows: "MCP servers are configured but not connected yet. Use Refresh Available Tools to discover configured MCP tools."
- Probe Tool is absent.

After clicking Refresh Available Tools:
- browser RPC request observed: tools.effective.refresh with { agentId: "main", sessionKey: "agent:main:main" }
- Probe Tool appears with source MCP.
```

Screenshots are hosted on a separate fork artifact branch, not committed to this PR diff:

<details open>
<summary>Current head cold read: configured-MCP notice visible; Probe Tool absent</summary>

![Current head cold Control UI MCP notice](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/f5cfb1a499a3-01-cold-mcp-notice.png)
</details>

<details open>
<summary>Current head after refresh: Probe Tool appears as an MCP tool</summary>

![Current head after refresh MCP tool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/f5cfb1a499a3-02-after-refresh-mcp-tool.png)
</details>

- Observed result after fix: a cold `tools.effective` read returns only core/plugin groups plus `mcp-not-yet-connected` and does not create a session MCP runtime; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes the configured stdio MCP server and returns `reproProbe__probe_tool`; the next `tools.effective` read shows the warm MCP group via peek without another cold materialization.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior; #80909; full cross-platform/browser E2E. This PR is scoped to the Gateway/dashboard effective-inventory boundary.
## Root Cause (if applicable)
- Root cause: `tools.effective` used the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` path, which builds from OpenClaw's built-in tools and plugin/channel sources. Bundled MCP tools are materialized later through the session MCP runtime/materializer, so dashboard inventory and runtime provider inventory diverged.
- Boundary correction: live MCP discovery/listing can start configured stdio or remote transports, so it now lives behind explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`. The read-scoped method only peeks an existing matching-fingerprint runtime catalog and returns notices otherwise.
- Missing guardrail: coverage did not lock cold-read side effects, refresh materialization, warm catalog projection, or stale MCP config fingerprints through the handler/RPC/UI path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Added/updated coverage for cold-read no-side-effect notices, warm MCP catalog projection, stale-fingerprint notices, explicit refresh materialization, Control UI read-vs-refresh behavior, and SDK/protocol exposure.
- Focused Gateway/unit/UI/SDK checks were run during the PR; current-head Gateway RPC and Control UI proof are included above.
- Live GitHub checks on current head show the earlier `check-test-types` failure fixed and no visible current-head CI failure remaining.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
The Agent Tools panel now loads without implicitly starting configured MCP transports. If MCP servers are configured but not connected/listed yet, the panel shows an explanatory notice and a **Refresh Available Tools** action. Triggering refresh performs live discovery and lets subsequent reads show the warm MCP tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)
```text
Before this PR:
[tools.effective] -> [static effective inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

Updated design:
[tools.effective]         -> [static inventory + peek existing MCP catalog] -> [core/plugin/channel + notices or warm MCP]
[tools.effective.refresh] -> [session MCP runtime materialize] -> [final bundled-tool policy] -> [core/plugin/channel + MCP]
```

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? No new capability beyond existing configured MCP materialization; yes, a new explicit Gateway method is added.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? Yes, only from explicit `tools.effective.refresh` or existing run-lifecycle MCP materialization, not from cold `tools.effective` reads.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, refresh can start/list configured MCP transports; it does not invoke MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: live MCP listing can start a configured stdio server or connect to a configured remote endpoint. This PR moves that side effect out of `operator.read` and into explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`; read-only inventory only peeks an existing runtime catalog with a matching config fingerprint. Gateway/security owner review is requested for this boundary before merge.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- Linux local dev, Node via `node --import tsx`, QA mock provider.
- Gateway WebSocket RPC proof: `sessions.create` -> cold `tools.effective` -> `tools.effective.refresh` -> warm `tools.effective`.
- Control UI browser proof: mock Gateway responses for UI rendering/dispatch; one stdio MCP server `reproProbe` exposes `probe_tool`.

### Steps
1. Run the Gateway proof harness against current PR head and assert cold/read, refresh, and warm/read states.
2. Run the Control UI browser proof, confirm the cold MCP notice, click **Refresh Available Tools**, and confirm `Probe Tool` appears as MCP.

### Expected
- Cold read: no MCP cold-start/listing; notice returned.
- Refresh: MCP listed/materialized through explicit write-scoped method.
- Warm read: MCP group visible through existing catalog peek.

### Actual
- Cold read: `runtimeCreated: false`, `containsMcpTarget: false`, `noticeIds: ["mcp-not-yet-connected"]`.
- Refresh: `runtimeCreated: true`, `matchingIds: ["reproProbe__probe_tool"]`, `catalogToolNames: ["probe_tool"]`.
- Warm read: `containsMcpTarget: true`, no stale not-connected notice.
- Control UI: cold notice screenshot and after-refresh MCP tool screenshot are linked above.

## Evidence
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)
- Current-head Gateway RPC proof passed on `f5cfb1a499a3`.
- Current-head Control UI browser proof captured the cold notice and after-refresh MCP tool screenshots.
- Public screenshot URLs were fetched back; hashes matched and OCR confirmed the expected visible text.
- `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` exited 0 against current `upstream/main` (`1bc32e53ab1d`).
- `gh pr view` shows current head `f5cfb1a499a30b5d2e091ea47f368d478192fb66`, `mergeStateStatus=CLEAN`, and current visible checks completed successfully/skipped/neutral.

## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: users may expect MCP tools to appear immediately on panel load.
  - Mitigation: the panel shows MCP notices and an explicit refresh action; warm catalogs still appear in read-only inventory.
- Risk: refresh can start configured MCP transports.
  - Mitigation: refresh is explicit and registered as `operator.write`; cold `operator.read` inventory no longer starts or lists MCP transports.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.
target.author: nxmxbbd
